Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 11:02 ET
Index puts surge 1.57 P/C while single-names favor calls 0.84
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. Dispersion trade setup — index hedging meets single-name optimism
  • 2. Negative gamma -$7.8B amplifies moves into Core PCE Thursday
  • 3. Energy breakout XLE score 91 contradicts broad market caution
NARRATIVE — dispersion hedge into PCE

Markets exhibit classic institutional dispersion positioning with index P/C at 1.57 while single-name P/C sits at 0.84 — a 56-point flow/regime divergence suggesting portfolio hedging alongside selective bullish bets. This split coincides with implied correlation at 0.37 (stock-specific regime) and massive IWM put blocks totaling 36,559 contracts across Mar/Apr strikes, confirming small-cap hedging demand. The CNN Fear & Greed at 20 (Extreme Fear) validates the index protection theme, yet VIX at 25.1 remains contained in contango (+0.44 term structure spread). Dealer positioning creates amplified risk into Thursday's Core PCE with SPY net gamma at -$7.8B and flip level $10 above spot at $669. This negative gamma environment means any PCE surprise triggers mechanical selling into weakness or buying into strength, independent of fundamental views. The technical divergence compounds this setup — energy leads with XLE at 91.0 technical score (golden cross, RSI 80) while mega-caps deteriorate with MSFT at 12.9 score (death cross, RSI 28). Institutional flows show strategic rotation with Millennium's MSFT conviction build (+135% Q4 to $2.49B) contrasting their AMZN reversal (+398% Q4 after Q3 reduction). ARM exhibits pure volatility demand with calls +276% and puts +111% simultaneously, while 13 names trade in backwardation suggesting elevated near-term event risk pricing ahead of the PCE catalyst.

Layer 1: P/C divergence 1.57/0.84Layer 2: GEX -$7.8BLayer 3: IWM put blocks 36kLayer 4: IC 0.37 stock-specificLayer 5: XLE 91 vs MSFT 12.9Layer 9: PCE Thursday

ATM IV = at-the-money implied vol  |  IV PCTILE = where today's IV ranks vs ≈90 trading days (green=cheap, red=expensive)  |  Spread = ATM IV − RV 20d  |  Rich: IV/RV >1.2×  |  Skew 90/110: P(90%) − C(110%)  |  SKEW PCTILE: vs ≈90d history  |  PUT/CALL V%: daily volume rank vs ≈90d history  |  vs SECTOR: ticker ATM IV / sector ETF ATM IV (±10% threshold)  |  Divergence: cross-signal flags (VOL HIGH/SKEW FLAT, CHEAP VOL/RICH SKEW, etc.)
